title: The Secret of Chimneys
description: The Secret of Chimneys — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Secret of Chimneys" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata
'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Abbotbury Mansions
memes.description: A London mansion block mentioned in connection with George Lomax's official business — part of the bureaucratic London landscape of the Foreign Office.

memes.label: Anthony Cade Accepts the Job
memes.description: In Bulawayo, South Africa, Jimmy McGrath offers Anthony Cade £1000 to deliver Count Stylptitch's coded memoirs to a London publisher, setting the entire plot in motion.

memes.label: Anthony Cade
memes.description: Adventurer and protagonist who becomes entangled in the Herzoslovakian conspiracy after agreeing to deliver Count Stylptitch's memoirs

memes.label: Anthony Cade's Return to England
memes.description: Anthony Cade's return to England from Bulawayo, travelling under Jimmy McGrath's name — the novel's inciting incident that places Anthony at the intersection of the Herzoslovakian conspiracy and the Chimneys murder.

memes.label: Assassination of Nicholas IV
memes.description: The assassination of King Nicholas IV of Herzoslovakia approximately seven years before the novel's events — the political earthquake that toppled the monarchy, established the republic, and set every faction in motion toward the Koh-i-Noor and Chimneys.

memes.label: Balderson and Hodgkins
memes.description: London publishing firm that holds Count Stylptitch's manuscript — the firm to which Anthony Cade delivers the memoirs and receives a cheque for one thousand pounds, setting the plot in motion.

memes.label: Balkans
memes.description: The volatile Balkan region that provides the political backdrop for the novel — home to Herzoslovakia, the scene of Count Stylptitch's diplomatic intrigues, and the source of the revolutionary Comrades of the Red Hand.

memes.label: Baron Lolopretjzyl
memes.description: Baron Lolopretjzyl is the London representative of the Herzoslovakian Loyalist party, a dignified and bearded diplomat who seeks to recover Count Stylptitch's memoirs and restore the monarchy under Prince Michael.

memes.label: Battle Arrives at Chimneys
memes.description: Superintendent Battle of Scotland Yard arrives at Chimneys to investigate the murder of Prince Michael Obolovitch, taking over from the local police.

memes.label: Bill Eversleigh
memes.description: Bill Eversleigh is George Lomax's private secretary at the Foreign Office, a good-natured but not particularly bright young man who runs errands and provides comic relief while nursing a romantic interest in Virginia Revel.

memes.label: Blackfriars Bridge
memes.description: A bridge over the Thames in London — the location where key evidence related to the Herzoslovakian conspiracy is disposed of during the investigation.

memes.label: Blitz Hotel
memes.description: London hotel where Anthony Cade stays upon arriving in England — the setting for his first encounter with the coded letters, the visit from Baron Lolopretjzyl, and the beginning of his entanglement in the Herzoslovakian affair.

memes.label: Boris Anchoukoff
memes.description: Boris Anchoukoff is the devoted Herzoslovakian valet of Prince Michael Obolovitch. A man of few words and doglike loyalty, he becomes an unlikely ally to Anthony Cade after his master's murder.

memes.label: British Embassy in Herzoslovakia
memes.description: The British diplomatic mission in Herzoslovakia where Virginia Revel's late husband Tim Revel was posted — giving Virginia her firsthand knowledge of Balkan politics that George Lomax exploits in recruiting her for the Chimneys conference.

memes.label: Bulawayo
memes.description: Bulawayo is a city in present-day Zimbabwe (then Southern Rhodesia), where the novel opens. Anthony Cade is working here as a tour guide for Castle's Select Tours when Jimmy McGrath arrives with Count Stylptitch's memoirs, setting the plot in motion.

memes.label: Burnham Beeches
memes.description: A forested area west of London where Anthony Cade hides a pistol—key evidence in the Chimneys mystery—in a niche in a large tree.

memes.label: Cadogan Square
memes.description: George Lomax's London residence — the setting for Lomax's anxious consultation with Bill Eversleigh about the Herzoslovakian succession crisis, and where the novel's diplomatic machinery is set in motion.

memes.label: Cape Town
memes.description: A major port city in South Africa, referenced in connection with Anthony Cade's African background and the Union Castle Line shipping route between Africa and England.

memes.label: Captain Andrassy
memes.description: Captain Andrassy is the equerry to Prince Michael Obolovitch of Herzoslovakia. A correct, formal military officer fiercely loyal to the royalist cause, he is initially suspicious of Anthony Cade but ultimately participates in the final confrontation.

memes.label: Captain O'Neill
memes.description: An alias used by King Victor in Paris — the name under which he operated when the forged love letters supposedly written by Virginia Revel were created, and a key link between the Paris underworld and the Chimneys conspiracy.

memes.label: Carnfrae Castle
memes.description: A Scottish castle mentioned as an alternative setting — one of several country houses that form the backdrop to the novel's aristocratic world, alongside Chimneys and Wyvern Abbey.

memes.description: "“Gentleman Joe!”"

memes.description: "“So that’s that,” said Anthony, finishing off his glass and replacing it on the table."

memes.description: "“Quite so, my dear fellow, quite so,” said Lord Caterham."

memes.label: "Chapter 4: Introducing a Very Charming Lady"
memes.description: "George Lomax returned straightway to Whitehall."

memes.label: "Chapter 5: First Night in London"
memes.description: "There is often a flaw in the best-laid plans."

memes.label: "Chapter 6: The Gentle Art of Blackmail"
memes.description: "It was exactly five minutes to four when Virginia Revel, rendered punctual by a healthy curiosity, returned to the house in Pont Street."

memes.description: "Superintendent Battle replaced the sheet with the slightly crestfallen air of a man whose best point has fallen flat."

memes.label: "Chapter 14: Mainly Political and Financial"

memes.label: "Chapter 15: The French Stranger"
memes.description: "Virginia and Anthony walked side by side down the path which led to the lake."

memes.label: "Chapter 16: Tea in the Schoolroom"
memes.description: "Anthony regained the terrace with the feeling uppermost in his mind that the only safe place for private conversations was the middle of the lake."

memes.label: "Chapter 17: A Midnight Adventure"
memes.description: "The inquest took place on the following morning."

memes.label: "Chapter 18: Second Midnight Adventure"
memes.description: "The first person that Anthony saw as he alighted from his train on the following afternoon was Superintendent Battle."

memes.label: "Chapter 19: Secret History"
memes.description: "They all stared at the French detective, who smiled back at them."

memes.label: "Chapter 20: Battle and Anthony Confer"
memes.description: "Anthony said nothing."

memes.description: "Superintendent Battle was standing in the library at Wyvern Abbey."

memes.label: "Chapter 23: Encounter in the Rose Garden"
memes.description: "At 2:30 a little party met together in the Council Chamber: Bundle, Virginia, Superintendent Battle, M."

memes.label: "Chapter 24: The House at Dover"

memes.label: "Chapter 25: Tuesday Night at Chimneys"
memes.description: "Lord Caterham, Virginia and Bundle were sitting in the library after dinner."

memes.label: "Chapter 26: The 13th of October"
memes.description: "At ten o’clock on the morning of Wednesday, the 13th of October, Anthony Cade walked into Harridge’s Hotel and asked for Baron Lolopretjzyl who was occupying a suite there."

memes.label: "Chapter 27: The 13th of October (Continued.)"
memes.description: "The clock in the Council Chamber struck nine."

memes.label: "Chapter 28: King Victor"
memes.description: "“I suspected her from the first,” explained Anthony."

memes.label: "Chapter 29: Further Explanations"
memes.description: "“You owe us an explanation, I think, Mr."

memes.label: "Chapter 30: Anthony Signs on for a New Job"
memes.description: "“If you will go on, gentlemen, I will follow you in a minute,” said Anthony."

memes.label: Character Index
memes.description: Complete index of all characters in The Secret of Chimneys, organized alphabetically with quality ratings.

memes.label: Chimneys
memes.description: The English country house owned by Lord Caterham, serving as the primary setting for much of the novel's action — the convergence point for international intrigue, murder investigation, and political conspiracy

memes.label: Colonel Melrose
memes.description: Colonel Melrose is the Chief Constable who assists Superintendent Battle in the investigation of Prince Michael Obolovitch's murder at Chimneys.

memes.label: Comrades of the Red Hand
memes.description: A ruthless Herzoslovakian revolutionary secret society known for political assassination — responsible for Prince Michael Obolovitch's murder and marked by their signature scarlet hand emblem.

memes.label: Concepts Index
memes.description: Complete index of all concepts, themes, and plot devices in The Secret of Chimneys.

memes.label: Council Chamber
memes.description: The Council Chamber is a historic oak-panelled room at Chimneys, formerly used for privy council meetings. It contains a secret passage behind a Holbein portrait and serves as the central setting for much of the novel's investigation and denouement.

memes.label: Count Stanislaus
memes.description: Count Stanislaus is the incognito under which Prince Michael Obolovitch of Herzoslovakia stays at Chimneys, where he is murdered on the first night of the house party.

memes.label: Count Stylptitch
memes.description: Count Stylptitch of Herzoslovakia, known as "the Grand Old Man of the Balkans," was a master statesman and intriguer whose posthumously published memoirs set in motion the events at Chimneys.
aliases: the Grand Old Man of the Balkans

memes.label: Count Stylptitch's Memoirs
memes.description: The explosive memoirs of Count Stylptitch, late prime minister of Herzoslovakia — a coded manuscript that contains dangerous political secrets, the key to the Koh-i-Noor diamond's whereabouts, and the hidden information that drives the entire plot of The Secret of Chimneys.

memes.label: Count Stylptitch's Political Legacy
memes.description: The enduring political influence of Count Stylptitch, the former prime minister of Herzoslovakia whose memoirs threaten to destabilize European diplomacy.
aliases: the grand old man of the Balkans

memes.label: Countess Popoffsky
memes.description: The fabricated noble identity — complete with a pretence of Romanov blood — that Nicholas IV created for his future wife Varaga to make her acceptable as Queen of Herzoslovakia.

memes.label: Crown Jewels
memes.description: The Crown Jewels of Herzoslovakia, many of which were discovered to have had their principal stones replaced by paste after the assassination of the king and queen. The substitution was traced to Angèle Mory, the former Parisian actress turned queen.

memes.label: Decoding the Memoirs
memes.description: Anthony Cade decodes Count Stylptitch's coded memoirs to reveal the secret contained within, after receiving them from Jimmy McGrath.

memes.label: Diplomatic Service
memes.description: The British Diplomatic Service, in which Virginia Revel's late husband served. His posting to Herzoslovakia connects the diplomatic corps to the novel's central political intrigue.

memes.label: Disposal of Prince Michael's Body
memes.description: Anthony Cade and Virginia Revel dispose of Prince Michael Obolovitch's body after discovering him shot dead in Virginia's study at Pont Street.

relations.label: The Discovery of Prince Michael's Body

memes.label: Dover
memes.description: English Channel port town where King Victor's headquarters — the house at Hurstmere, Langly Road — is located, serving as the setting for Chapter XXIV.

memes.label: Ekarest
memes.description: The capital city of Herzoslovakia, site of the royal cathedral where King Nicholas IV married Queen Varaga.

memes.label: Ekarest Cathedral
memes.description: The cathedral in Ekarest, Herzoslovakia — where King Nicholas IV married the former Parisian music-hall performer who became Queen Varaga, a union that helped precipitate the revolution.

memes.label: England
memes.description: The primary setting of the novel — the country to which Anthony Cade returns from Africa, where Chimneys and its weekend parties host diplomatic history, and where the Koh-i-Noor intrigue unfolds.

memes.label: Events Index
memes.description: Complete index of all key events in The Secret of Chimneys — crimes, political intrigues, and social occasions.

memes.label: Folies Bergère
memes.description: The Parisian music hall where Angèle Mory performed — a venue connected to King Victor's criminal network through Mory's association with the Comrades of the Red Hand.

memes.label: Foreign Office
memes.description: The British Foreign Office, represented by the Hon. George Lomax, is the government department orchestrating the royalist restoration in Herzoslovakia — and the force that draws Chimneys into international politics.

memes.label: George Lomax
memes.description: A senior British politician and Foreign Office official who orchestrates the diplomatic conference at Chimneys to secure Prince Michael's restoration to the Herzoslovakian throne.

memes.label: Granarth Castle
memes.description: The Union Castle liner on which James McGrath (actually Anthony Cade) traveled from South Africa to England, arriving at Southampton. A key plot device in the novel's opening.

memes.label: Harley Street
memes.description: A prestigious London street known for its medical consulting rooms. Sir Abner Willis, Lord Caterham's physician, and Edward Henry Revel, a surgeon sharing Virginia Revel's surname, both have addresses here.

memes.label: Herman Isaacstein
memes.description: Herman Isaacstein is a powerful international financier and representative of a British oil syndicate seeking concessions in Herzoslovakia, making him a key figure in the political intrigue at Chimneys.

memes.label: Herzoslovakia
memes.description: A fictional Balkan kingdom — once a monarchy under the Obolovitch dynasty, now a republic riven by revolutionary factions and oil concessions, the political fulcrum of the novel's intrigue.

memes.label: Herzoslovakian Oil Concession
memes.description: The rumoured oil discovery in Herzoslovakia and the competing financial concessions that drive the political manoeuvring in the novel.

memes.label: Herzoslovakian Revolution
memes.description: The revolution that overthrew King Nicholas IV of Herzoslovakia, assassinated the king and his controversial queen Varaga, and established the republic — the political earthquake whose aftershocks drive the novel's plot.

memes.label: Herzoslovakian Revolutionaries
memes.description: The republican/revolutionary political faction that overthrew the Herzoslovakian monarchy — allied with the Comrades of the Red Hand and opposed to the Loyalist (Royalist) party's restoration efforts.

memes.label: Herzoslovakian Royalists
memes.description: The monarchist political faction seeking the restoration of the Herzoslovakian throne — represented in London by Baron Lolopretjzyl and competing against the Revolutionary party for control of the nation's future.

memes.label: Hiram Fish
memes.description: Hiram Fish, an American guest at Chimneys who presents himself as a collector of rare books but proves to be a skilled investigator tracking King Victor.

memes.label: Hiram Fish's Arrival at Chimneys
memes.description: American visitor Hiram Fish arrives at Chimneys ostensibly as a weekend guest, later revealed to be a Pinkerton detective investigating the Herzoslovakian affair.

memes.label: Hurstmere
memes.description: A house on Langly Road in Dover, used as a hideout and headquarters by the King Victor gang.

memes.label: Inspector Badgworthy
memes.description: Inspector Badgworthy is the local police inspector at Market Basing who is the first official to respond to the murder at Chimneys, though his investigation is soon superseded by Scotland Yard.

memes.label: James McGrath
memes.description: James "Jimmy" McGrath is a Canadian adventurer and journalist, a friend of Anthony Cade who entrusts him with Count Stylptitch's memoirs and letters, setting the plot of The Secret of Chimneys in motion.

memes.label: King Victor
memes.description: King Victor — the infamous international jewel thief, leader of a Paris-based criminal gang, whose operations intersect with the Herzoslovakian affair, the Koh-i-Noor diamond, and the murder at Chimneys.

memes.label: King Victor's Unmasking
memes.description: The climactic scene in the Council Chamber at Chimneys in which the true identity of the international jewel thief King Victor is dramatically revealed before the assembled house party — unmasking a figure who has deceived everyone throughout the investigation.
aliases: the revelation of King Victor's identity

memes.label: Koh-i-Noor Diamond
memes.description: The legendary diamond — referred to obliquely throughout the novel as "the K" or "the historic whatnot" — which was stolen during a diplomatic gathering at Chimneys years earlier and whose hidden location drives the criminal conspiracy at the heart of the plot.

memes.label: Lady Eileen "Bundle" Brent
memes.description: Lady Eileen Brent, universally known as "Bundle," is the eldest daughter of Lord Caterham and a spirited, sharp-witted young woman who assists Anthony Cade in the investigation at Chimneys.

memes.label: London
memes.description: London serves as the novel's urban setting — the scene of political maneuvering, blackmail attempts, and the initial investigation before the action moves to Chimneys.

memes.label: Lord Caterham
memes.description: Clement Edward Alistair Brent, 9th Marquis of Caterham — the reluctant owner of Chimneys, a shabbily-dressed aristocrat who detests politics and politicians, and wants nothing more than to be left in peace.

memes.label: Lunch with Lord Caterham
memes.description: An awkward lunch at Chimneys during the investigation, where the heterogeneous assembly of guests included diplomats, detectives, and suspects.

memes.label: Mademoiselle Brun
memes.description: Mademoiselle Brun is the French governess at Chimneys, later revealed to be Angèle Mory, a former Folies Bergères actress who became Queen Varaga of Herzoslovakia and an accomplice of the notorious jewel thief King Victor.

memes.label: Matoppos
memes.description: A scenic destination near Bulawayo in Southern Rhodesia (present-day Zimbabwe), mentioned in the novel's opening chapter as the intended excursion for Anthony Cade's tour group with Castle's Select Tour.

memes.label: Miss Oscar
memes.description: Miss Oscar is George Lomax's efficient secretary, managing his appointments, correspondence, and telephone calls from the Foreign Office. Though she never appears directly in the narrative, she is a constant behind-the-scenes presence facilitating Lomax's political machinations.

memes.label: Miss Taylor
memes.description: A young woman in Anthony Cade's tourist group in Bulawayo, South Africa. The youngest and most vivacious of the party, she flirts with Anthony during the novel's opening scene.

memes.label: Monsieur Lemoine
memes.description: Monsieur Lemoine is a detective from the Sûreté in Paris, sent to work with Superintendent Battle on the Herzoslovakian case. A clever and observant investigator, his true identity and allegiance become central to the novel's resolution.

memes.label: Mr. Isaacstein's Suitcase
memes.description: Mr. Isaacstein's suitcase falls from a luggage cart at Chimneys, leading to a covert search that reveals hidden documents — witnessed by Bill Eversleigh.

memes.label: Murder at Chimneys
memes.description: The central crime of the novel — a mysterious death at the Chimneys country house that brings Scotland Yard into the Herzoslovakian conspiracy

memes.label: Nicholas IV
memes.description: Nicholas IV was the last King of Herzoslovakia, assassinated approximately seven years before the events of the novel. His marriage to the low-born Angèle Mory (Queen Varaga) and subsequent assassination triggered the political instability that forms the backdrop of The Secret of Chimneys.
aliases: His Most Gracious Majesty King Nicholas IV

memes.label: Obolovitch Dynasty
memes.description: The royal house of Herzoslovakia — the ruling dynasty whose deposed and scattered members are at the center of the novel's political intrigue, with claimants, pretenders, and revolutionaries contesting the future of the Balkan kingdom.

memes.label: Organizations Index
memes.description: Complete index of all organizations in The Secret of Chimneys — from Scotland Yard to revolutionary secret societies.

memes.label: Paddington Station
memes.description: London railway terminus from which Virginia Revel departs for Chimneys, and where Anthony Cade executes the trunk exchange scheme with the Koh-i-Noor diamond.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: Paris is the city where much of the novel's backstory unfolds — the base of operations for the international jewel thief King Victor, the home of the Sûreté, and the setting for Angèle Mory's rise from music-hall performer to queen.

memes.label: Pinkerton
memes.description: The American private detective agency that employs Hiram P. Fish to trail the international jewel thief King Victor from America to England.

memes.label: Places Index
memes.description: Complete index of all locations in The Secret of Chimneys, from grand estates to foreign capitals.

memes.label: Pont Street
memes.description: Virginia Revel's London residence at 487 Pont Street — the setting for the blackmail attempt, the discovery of Giuseppe Manelli's body, and several key conversations.

memes.label: Prince Michael Obolovitch
memes.description: Prince Michael Obolovitch of Herzoslovakia, a claimant to the Herzoslovakian throne who is murdered at Chimneys under mysterious circumstances.

memes.label: Prince Michael Obolovitch's Arrival in England
memes.description: Prince Michael Obolovitch arrives in England to claim the throne of Herzoslovakia, setting the political plot in motion — his presence triggers the diplomatic conference at Chimneys and his murder days later becomes the central crime of the novel.

memes.label: Prince Nicholas Obolovitch
memes.description: Prince Nicholas Obolovitch is the first cousin and legitimate heir to the Herzoslovakian throne after the death of Prince Michael, though his identity is exploited by the master criminal King Victor for a sophisticated impersonation scheme.

memes.label: Professor Wynward
memes.description: Professor Wynward is a cipher expert summoned to Chimneys to decode the secret correspondence between Queen Varaga and King Victor. His decryption work provides crucial evidence in the investigation.

memes.label: Queen Varaga
memes.description: Queen Varaga, born Angèle Mory, was a Parisian actress and professional thief who became the Queen of Herzoslovakia through her marriage to King Nicholas IV. Her criminal past and low birth triggered the revolution that killed both monarchs, and her role in the theft of the Koh-i-Noor diamond is central to the novel's mystery.

memes.label: Ranelagh
memes.description: London social and sporting venue where Virginia Revel plays tennis on the afternoon of Giuseppe Manelli's blackmail attempt, providing her alibi for the key events that follow.

memes.label: Red Hand
memes.description: The Comrades of the Red Hand — a revolutionary republican organization in Herzoslovakia opposed to the royalist restoration, suspected of involvement in the murder of Prince Michael Obolovitch at Chimneys.

memes.label: Restoration of the Herzoslovakian Monarchy
memes.description: The political project to restore the Obolovitch monarchy in Herzoslovakia following the revolution—the ostensible justification for the diplomatic conference at Chimneys.

memes.label: Romanov Blood
memes.description: The false claim — fabricated by Nicholas IV — that his bride Varaga possessed Romanov blood, part of the Countess Popoffsky deception designed to legitimize her as Queen of Herzoslovakia.

memes.label: Rose Garden
memes.description: The Rose Garden at Chimneys is the setting for Anthony Cade's crucial realization about the hidden jewel's location. Its sundial and roses provide the clue that unlocks the mystery of Count Stylptitch's cryptic message.

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: The headquarters of the London Metropolitan Police, represented in the novel by Superintendent Battle investigating the murder at Chimneys

memes.label: Select Tours
memes.description: A travel-tour front company operated by the criminal figure Castle — mentioned by Anthony Cade as the kind of dubious enterprise he has encountered in his adventures.

memes.label: South Africa
memes.description: The novel's opening setting where Anthony Cade and James McGrath both reside before the events of the story. McGrath owns a farm and Cade worked as a tourist guide for Messrs. Castle.

memes.label: Southampton
memes.description: The English port where the Granarth Castle — the ship carrying Anthony Cade from Bulawayo — docked two days earlier than expected due to a clerical error at the Union Castle Line office.

memes.label: Staines
memes.description: Staines is a location on the road between Hounslow and Staines where Anthony Cade disposes of Giuseppe's body and where a shooting incident later occurs. It represents the novel's shadow world of gangland violence intruding upon the English countryside.

memes.label: Superintendent Battle
memes.description: Superintendent Battle of Scotland Yard — a squarely built, inscrutable detective whose face is "singularly devoid of expression," called to investigate the murder at Chimneys.

memes.label: Sûreté
memes.description: The French national detective police force based in Paris — the agency that employs M. Lemoine and tracks the international jewel thief King Victor across Europe.

memes.label: Tea in the Schoolroom
memes.description: Anthony Cade takes tea in the schoolroom at Chimneys with Lady Eileen "Bundle" Brent and Mademoiselle Brun, during which key revelations about the case emerge.

memes.label: The Blackmail of Virginia Revel
memes.description: Giuseppe Manelli's attempt to blackmail Virginia Revel at 487 Pont Street using forged love letters — an act that sets in motion the chain of events leading to his death at Chimneys.

memes.label: The British Government's Involvement
memes.description: The British government's diplomatic and political involvement in the Herzoslovakian succession crisis, orchestrated through George Lomax and the Foreign Office.

memes.label: The Code Letters
memes.description: The secret coded letters written by Angèle Mory (as Queen Varaga) to King Victor, which contain the hidden location of the Koh-i-Noor diamond and drive the central mystery.

memes.label: The Council Chamber at Chimneys
memes.description: The oak-panelled Council Chamber at Chimneys, a room of historical and political significance that serves as the focal point for the novel's central mysteries.

memes.label: The Council Chamber Showdown
memes.description: The climactic confrontation in the Council Chamber at Chimneys where King Victor's true identity is exposed and the Herzoslovakian conspiracy unravels.

memes.label: The Denouement at Chimneys
memes.description: Following the unmasking of King Victor, Anthony Cade provides a comprehensive explanation of how he solved the case, connecting all the threads of the mystery.

memes.label: The Diplomatic Conference at Chimneys
memes.description: George Lomax's secret diplomatic conference held at Chimneys on October 13th, intended to settle the future of Herzoslovakia and the Balkans — a meeting that provides the political backdrop for the novel's crimes and reveals the true stakes of the Koh-i-Noor diamond.

memes.label: The Discovery of Prince Michael's Body
memes.description: The moment Virginia Revel returns to her Pont Street residence on a June evening to find Prince Michael Obolovitch shot dead in her study — a discovery that robs her of her composure, draws Anthony Cade into the case, and sets the murder investigation in motion.

memes.label: The Dover Expedition
memes.description: Anthony Cade's journey to Dover to infiltrate King Victor's headquarters at Hurstmere, Langly Road — a daring solo mission that brings him face to face with the Comrades of the Red Hand and leads to the revelation of Hiram Fish's true identity.

memes.label: The Foreign Office Arrangement
memes.description: George Lomax persuades Lord Caterham at their London club to host the secret diplomatic conference on Herzoslovakia at Chimneys, setting the stage for the weekend house party that frames the novel's events.

memes.label: The Hotel Room Raid
memes.description: Members of the Comrades of the Red Hand attempt to steal Count Stylptitch's memoirs from Anthony Cade's room at the Blitz Hotel in London.

memes.label: The House Party at Chimneys
memes.description: The weekend house party at Chimneys that brings together diplomats, aristocrats, adventurers, and criminals — the crucible in which the novel's intersecting conspiracies collide.

memes.label: The Jolly Cricketers
memes.description: A local inn near Chimneys where strangers stay during the investigation; described by Lord Caterham as a "horrible place to stay."

memes.label: The Memoir Delivery
memes.description: Jimmy McGrath delivers Count Stylptitch's coded memoirs to Anthony Cade in Bulawayo, launching the central plot of the novel.

memes.label: The Memoir Packet
memes.description: The oilskin packet containing Count Stylptitch's coded memoirs, passed from Dutch Pedro to Jimmy McGrath and then to Anthony Cade.

memes.label: The Midnight Adventures at Chimneys
memes.description: Two consecutive nights in which Bill Eversleigh and Virginia Revel investigate mysterious intruders dismantling suits of armour in the Council Chamber at Chimneys — uncovering clues about a hidden passage and the search for the stolen jewel.
aliases: two nights of investigation at Chimneys

memes.label: The Oil Concession Negotiations
memes.description: The financial and political negotiations over Herzoslovakian oil rights involving Prince Michael, Herman Isaacstein, the British government, and competing American interests — the economic backdrop to the diplomatic conference at Chimneys.

memes.label: The Poacher Shot
memes.description: A mysterious gunshot heard near Chimneys during the night, initially dismissed as a poacher in the woods but later revealed to have significance in the investigation.

memes.label: The Priest's Hole
memes.description: A hidden chamber and secret passage at Chimneys estate, accessible from the White Gallery, used as a hiding place during historical religious persecution.

memes.label: The Recovery of the Stolen Memoirs
memes.description: The multi-sided pursuit of Count Stylptitch's coded memoirs, stolen from Anthony Cade's hotel room, triggering a race between the Comrades of the Red Hand, the Loyalist party, and British intelligence.

memes.label: The Red Signal
memes.description: Boris Anchoukoff sends a coded red-light signal from the grounds of Chimneys to warn King Victor of imminent danger, using the pre-arranged system of the Comrades of the Red Hand.

memes.label: The Rose Garden Encounter
memes.description: Anthony Cade has a mysterious nighttime encounter in the Rose Garden at Chimneys, where a coded exchange takes place that advances the plot.
aliases: the midnight meeting at the Rose Garden

memes.label: The Search for King Victor
memes.description: The police search for the true identity of King Victor, the notorious international thief, during the investigation at Chimneys.

memes.label: The Secret History Revelation
memes.description: The French detective Balderson reveals the secret history of Prince Michael Obolovitch, explaining his true connection to Herzoslovakia and the circumstances of his death.

memes.label: The Secret of Chimneys
memes.description: The central mystery of the novel — the hidden secret connected to the Chimneys estate and the Herzoslovakian political intrigue

memes.label: The Succession Crisis
memes.description: The political crisis surrounding the succession to the throne of Herzoslovakia following the assassination of King Nicholas IV and the revolution.

memes.label: Theft of the Koh-i-Noor
memes.description: The mysterious disappearance — and apparent theft — of the legendary Koh-i-Noor diamond from Chimneys years before the novel's events, a scandal that was hushed up at the highest levels and whose secrets may be revealed in Count Stylptitch's memoirs.

memes.label: Tim Revel
memes.description: Tim Revel was Virginia Revel's late husband, an Irish diplomat attached to the British Embassy in Herzoslovakia who died before the events of the novel, leaving Virginia a "wicked widow."

memes.label: Tredwell
memes.description: Tredwell is the dignified butler at Chimneys, Lord Caterham's country estate. Silent, observant, and impeccably correct, he runs the household with quiet efficiency and serves as a witness to much of the intrigue that unfolds.

memes.label: Union Castle Line
memes.description: The shipping company operating the *Granarth Castle* passenger liner on the South Africa—Southampton route. George Lomax sends Bill Eversleigh to the Union Castle offices to check the ship's arrival, leading to the critical name miscommunication.

memes.label: Varaga Palace
memes.description: The royal palace in Ekarest, Herzoslovakia — stormed by revolutionaries during the uprising that murdered King Nicholas IV and Queen Varaga, leading to the proclamation of the republic.

memes.label: Virginia Revel
memes.description: Virginia Revel, a charming and resourceful widow who becomes entangled in the political intrigue at Chimneys and a key ally to Anthony Cade.

memes.label: Waterloo Station
memes.description: The London railway terminus where Anthony Cade arrives on the boat train after docking at Southampton, having traveled from Bulawayo under the alias of Jimmy McGrath.

memes.label: White Gallery
memes.description: A room on the upper floor of Chimneys estate containing a secret passage known as the Priest's Hole.

memes.label: Wyvern Abbey
memes.description: George Lomax's country estate, neighbouring Chimneys — connected by a historic secret passage, and the setting for Superintendent Battle's report to George Lomax.
